factors.summary: The company's debt leverage is stable with a slight increase in total debt, reflecting a focus on maintaining a strong financial position. While there is a decrease in debt related to ME&T, the Financial Products segment saw an increase. The company is managing its financial position through deleveraging and refinancing at lower rates, which has contributed to lower interest expense despite higher borrowing rates.

evidence.text: Total debt as of September 30, 2024 was $37.90 billion, an increase of $23 million from year-end 2023. Debt related to ME&T decreased $886 million in the first nine months of 2024 while debt related to Financial Products increased $966 million.

Our short-term priorities for the use of cash may vary from time to time as business needs and conditions dictate, our long-term cash deployment strategy is focused on maintaining a strong financial position in support of a mid-A rating.

Interest expense excluding Financial Products in the third quarter of 2024 was $125 million, compared with $129 million in the third quarter of 2023. The decrease was due to lower average debt outstanding, partially offset by higher average borrowing rates.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are having a mixed impact on the company's performance, with both favorable and unfavorable effects observed across different regions. Latin America experienced unfavorable currency impacts, particularly related to the Brazilian real, partially offsetting higher sales volumes. Conversely, in Asia/Pacific, unfavorable exchange impacts contributed to a decrease in sales volumes. Overall, while some regions are negatively affected, others have seen positive influences, leading to a mixed outcome.

evidence.text: Unfavorable foreign currency impacts were more than offset by favorable impacts from pension and other postemployment benefit (OPEB) plan costs and higher investment and interest income.

In Latin America, sales increased mainly due to higher sales volume, partially offset by unfavorable currency impacts, primarily related to the Brazilian real. The increase in sales volume was primarily driven by the impact from changes in dealer inventories. Dealer inventory increased during the third quarter of 2024, compared with a decrease during the third quarter of 2023.
evidence.subsection: Sales and Revenues by Geographic Region

evidence.text: In Asia/Pacific, sales decreased 7 percent due to lower sales volume. The decrease in sales volume was mainly driven by the impact from changes in dealer inventories. Dealer inventory decreased during the third quarter of 2024, compared with an increase during the third quarter of 2023.
